sections.heading: Your Productivity & Wellness Assessment
sections.body: This assessment helps you evaluate your current habits, stress levels, and productivity systems. Use it as a baseline — then retake it in 3 months to measure your progress. Answer honestly; this is for your eyes only.

sections.heading: Time Management Assessment
sections.body: Rate yourself 1-5 on each statement (1 = never, 5 = always):

code.snippet:
TIME MANAGEMENT:
□ I use a system to prioritize tasks (Eisenhower, etc.)    (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I time block my calendar for deep work                    (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I complete my top priority before checking email/Slack     (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I finish most tasks within estimated time                 (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I review my week and plan next week                       (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

FOCUS:
□ I use a focus technique (Pomodoro, etc.)                  (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I can work for 25+ minutes without checking my phone      (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I batch similar tasks (all reviews together, etc.)        (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I say no to non-essential meetings                        (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I minimize context switching between tasks                 (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

GOALS & HABITS:
□ I have written SMART goals for this quarter               (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I track my progress toward my goals                       (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I have consistent daily routines                          (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I use habit stacking to build new habits                  (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I review and adjust my goals quarterly                    (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

TOTAL PRODUCTIVITY SCORE: ___/75
20-35: Foundation needed — start with time blocking + Pomodoro
36-55: Good base — optimize with weekly reviews + goal tracking
56-75: Strong system — focus on consistency + continuous improvement
code.explanation: This isn't a pass/fail test — it's a diagnostic. Low scores in one area tell you where to focus your improvement efforts.

code.snippet:
STRESS MANAGEMENT:
□ I recognize my stress triggers                           (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I use breathing techniques when stressed                  (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I practice mindfulness or meditation regularly            (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I can disconnect from work after hours                    (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I have healthy coping mechanisms for stress               (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

PHYSICAL HEALTH:
□ I sleep 7-9 hours consistently                           (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I exercise 3+ times per week                             (1-2-3-4-5)
□ My workspace is ergonomic                                (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I eat balanced meals (protein, vegetables, etc.)          (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I take regular breaks during work                        (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

WORK-LIFE BALANCE:
□ I have clear boundaries between work and personal time    (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I have time for hobbies and relationships                 (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I don't check work email/Slack after hours                (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I feel energized at the end of most workdays              (1-2-3-4-5)
□ I take vacations and actually disconnect                  (1-2-3-4-5)
Score: ___/25

TOTAL WELLNESS SCORE: ___/75
20-35: Needs attention — prioritize sleep + stress management
36-55: Decent foundation — focus on consistency + boundaries
56-75: Strong wellness — maintain and optimize
code.explanation: Wellness scores often reveal hidden problems. Many developers score high on productivity but low on wellness — which is unsustainable long-term.

jargon.definition: Your starting measurement before making changes. Essential for tracking improvement over time.
When wellness scores are consistently low (below 35/75). Requires immediate attention to prevent health consequences.

summary: This assessment evaluates your productivity (time management, focus, goals) and wellness (stress, physical health, work-life balance) on a 1-5 scale. Scores below 35 in any area indicate it needs immediate attention. Retake in 3 months to measure progress.
